ogTitle: Smithlea vs Smoky Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Smithlea and Smoky Creek.
heading: Smithlea vs Smoky Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smithlea, Queensland and Smoky Creek, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smoky Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Smithlea (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Smithlea or Smoky Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smithlea has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Smoky Creek scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smithlea scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Smoky Creek scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smithlea or Smoky Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smoky Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
